frictions.description: Live rerun was initially blocked by environment drift: earlier evidence mixed stale fixtures with mismatched listeners, the app side was still inheriting a `ws://...` broadcast URL for server-side fetches, and the isolated validator stack only stabilized once both ports and runtime vars were pinned explicitly.
frictions.resolution: Normalized the server-side broadcast publish URL so `ws:`/`wss:` config can still be used for websocket clients, restarted a coherent local stack (`web:3100`, `broadcast:2999`) with aligned runtime vars, and captured a browser-surface terminal daemon-event ack plus websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` delivery with a measured `112.9ms` POST-to-frame delta.

appliedUpdates.description: Added non-production test-context daemon auth seam gated by internal shared secret and explicit test headers; production auth remains unchanged.
Added test coverage for test-auth acceptance/rejection and terminal flow broadcast with refetch:["delivery-loop"].
Upgraded VAL-CROSS-001 report with browser-backed live evidence: daemon-event 200 ack + websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` capture at `112.9ms`.

flowReports.summary: Daemon terminal events trigger broadcast with delivery-loop refetch wiring in daemon-event route.
No-spam behavior remains protected by material-change checks and route dedup/idempotency tests.

Authentication blocker is removed in test context and proven live: a browser-surface terminal v2 daemon-event under test-auth acknowledged on `web:3100` and emitted websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` on `broadcast:2999` within the 500ms timing contract.
evidence: pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/app/api/daemon-event/route.test.ts
